title: Jhol Mo:Mo (Soup Mo:Mo)

ingredients.title: For the Jhol (Soup Broth)
items: Mustard oil – 1 tbsp (for authentic Nepali flavor)
Soy sauce – ½ tsp (optional, modern twist)
Warm water or light chicken stock – 2 cups

instruction.title: Roast Base Ingredients
steps: Dry roast sesame seeds, peanuts, and chilies until aromatic.

instruction.title: Blend
steps: In a blender, combine roasted items with tomatoes, onion, garlic, ginger, salt, and water/stock.
Blend into a smooth soup-like texture.

instruction.title: Cook Jhol
steps: Heat mustard oil in a pan, pour in the blended mixture, and simmer for 10–15 minutes on low heat until aroma deepens.
Let the oil slightly separate and adjust consistency to a thick but pourable broth.

instruction.title: Season
steps: Add Timur, soy sauce, and lemon juice.
Taste and adjust salt and spice as needed.

instruction.title: Assemble Jhol Mo:Mo
steps: Place 6–8 steamed momos in a deep bowl.
Pour hot jhol (broth) over them generously.
Garnish with fresh coriander and a drizzle of chili oil.

instruction.title: Presentation Tips
steps: Use a deep stone or wooden bowl for rustic appeal.
Float sesame seeds or chopped herbs on top.
Serve immediately so momos remain juicy and not over-soaked.

chefTips: Roasting sesame seeds and chilies deeply enhances flavor.
Mustard oil gives authentic Nepali aroma—don’t skip if possible.
Adjust thickness: jhol should coat momos but still be drinkable.
Fresh timur is key for authentic tingling spice flavor.
